business.headline: Axis Bank appoints ex-Bandhan Bank finance chief Rajeev Mantri as CFOMantri will assume charge on September 28, 2026, subject to the necessary regulatory and statutory approvals, the bank said in a regulatory filing.

JPMorgan just posted record profit. How big is the world's biggest bank, really?JPMorgan Chase posted a record second-quarter 2026 profit as revenue rose across every business line. The results renewed focus on the bank's vast scale, from market value to daily payment flows.

India may get Rs 10 and 20 plastic currency notes from next year. Here's whyThe Reserve Bank of India has begun preparations for a pilot of polymer banknotes by inviting global bids for substrate supply. The trial, likely to start with Rs 10 and Rs 20 notes, will shape any wider rollout.

SpiceJet fined Rs 1 lakh for misleading consent tactics, loyalty programme opt-insA consumer watchdog finds SpiceJet's booking interface nudged users into loyalty enrolment and promotional consent, raising fresh questions over how digital platforms secure customer approval.
Reliance Q1 profit falls 22% despite 25% jump in revenue. Here's whyReliance Industries posted a 22% drop in first-quarter net profit even as revenue rose sharply.

Founder's life is hard: $1.2 billion fintech CEO says bank rejected home loanDespite a long relationship with the lender, a strong credit score and significant assets, a fintech founder said a leading private sector bank rejected his home loan application.

Apple beats Nvidia to become world's most valuable companyApple's market capitalisation stood at around $4.88 trillion on Friday as its shares remained largely steady. Nvidia, meanwhile, slipped to around $4.86 trillion after its shares fell 3.5%.

Awareness, better policies can unlock India's tourism potential, say expertsSpeaking at the India Today's FAITH Tourism Conclave 2026, tourism industry representatives said India should focus on promoting year-round travel, creating greater awareness about unique destinations and ensuring stable policies to support the sector.

When no asset looks an easy bet, do multi-asset allocation funds make sense?Equity markets have faced a sharp correction amid geopolitical tensions, while gold and silver, traditionally considered safe havens during periods of uncertainty, have also fallen sharply from the all-time highs they touched in January.

Fake bosses, real losses: Sebi warns of scammers posing as CEOs in rising boss scamSebi has warned companies about a rising 'boss scam' in which fraudsters impersonate senior executives to trick employees into transferring funds, and urged firms to strengthen their security mechanisms and enhance vigilance.

F1 could return to India in 2028 as government races to fix old tax hurdlesThe government has begun talks with stakeholders to revive Formula One in India by 2028. The push centres on fixing old tax and regulatory hurdles that derailed the Indian Grand Prix.

VFS Global continues to invest in AI to streamline visa application processingSpeaking at India Today's FAITH Tourism Conclave 2026, Yummi Talwar, COO, South Asia, VFS Global, said visa demand has largely returned to normal after the post-pandemic surge.

Sensex ends nearly 1,000 points up: 3 reasons why stock market jumped todaySensex and Nifty surged on Friday as IT stocks, Reliance Industries and private banks led buying. The S&P BSE Sensex was up 953.76 points, or 1.24%, at 78,140.63 around 3:07 pm, after touching an intraday high of 78,282.55.

8th Pay Commission: How much HRA could you get under different fitment factors?The HRA debate comes at an important stage in the 8th Pay Commission process. It has been more than seven months since the Commission was constituted. The Commission visited Kolkata, West Bengal, on July 9 and 10, 2026, as part of its ongoing consultation exercise.

Your ITR could be wrong even with Form 16: 7 smart checks to avoid tax noticesThink your Form 16 is all you need to file your ITR? Think again. A few missed details could delay your refund, or even land you a tax notice.

Maruti challenges landmark E20 ruling, says contaminated fuel was the real issueMaruti Suzuki will challenge the Raipur consumer court order on a Grand Vitara E20 dispute. The case sharpens scrutiny of whether alleged damage came from ethanol-blended petrol or contaminated fuel.

Why investors are betting against Elon Musk's SpaceXSome investors are concerned about the company's heavy investment in artificial intelligence, much of which is being financed through debt. These concerns have added pressure on the stock in recent weeks.

EPFO plans PF scheme for self-employed and unorganised workersIf implemented, the proposed scheme could significantly expand India's social security system by giving millions of self-employed individuals and workers in the unorganised sector an opportunity to save for retirement through a trusted and structured provident fund mechanism.

Trump posts, markets move. His company now wants to turn that into a businessTrump Media plans to launch Truth PSI, a paid service offering early access to Truth Social posts. The proposal has prompted criticism over possible market advantage and conflict-of-interest concerns.

Infosys, TCS, Tech Mahindra, HCLTech rally 2%; Why are IT stocks rising today?Tech Mahindra's stronger June-quarter earnings lifted IT shares, with Infosys, TCS and HCLTech joining the rally while Wipro fell. The divergent moves highlighted improving confidence in stronger performers as investors watched the sector's earnings season.

entertainment.headline: Why Ranbir Kapoor's Ramayana needs to conquer China to win the box office war Buzz around Ramayana Part 1 exploring the massive China market is growing. If the makers crack the deal, it will take the Ranbir Kapoor film's box office game to a new level.

The Odyssey box office Day 2: Saturday rush takes Nolan film near Rs 40 crore Director Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey posted a strong jump at the Indian box office on its second day. Positive word-of-mouth, IMAX demand and a solid English-language response kept its momentum intact.

Hrithik Roshan backs Sonam Wangchuk, says students' struggles sound true Actor Hrithik Roshan has extended support to activist Sonam Wangchuk amid his indefinite hunger strike over alleged examination irregularities. The actor shared Wangchuk's message on social media and said students' struggles resonated with him.

Sunny Deol confirms Lord Hanuman role in Ramayana, says he's just started filming Sunny Deol surprised fans at the Ramayana trailer launch and confirmed that he will portray Lord Hanuman in Nitesh Tiwari's epic saga. The actor called the opportunity one of the biggest honours of his career and shared his excitement about bringing the beloved character to life.

Our stars aligned: Jennifer Winget marries William Ishmael, shares wedding glimpses Actor Jennifer Winget got married to Singapore-based businessman William Ishmael in an intimate wedding ceremony. The actor shared dreamy pictures from her special day, giving fans a glimpse of her elegant bridal look and romantic celebration.

72nd National Film Awards full winners list: Best Actor, Best Film and more The 72nd National Film Awards were announced in New Delhi on Saturday, with Article 370 named Best Film. The list also recognised performers and filmmakers across feature and non-feature categories for CBFC-certified 2024 releases.

Choreographer Vijay Ganguly says National Award for Aaj Ki Raat is family milestone Vijay Ganguly has won the National Award for Best Choreography for Aaj Ki Raat from Stree 2. He said the honour is special because it brings a third National Award into his family.

It's a performance for the ages: Kabir Khan on Kartik Aaryan's National Award Director Kabir Khan said he called actor Kartik Aaryan after learning he had won his first National Award for Chandu Champion. In an exclusive interview with India Today, the director said Kartik's physical and mental transformation made the honour feel like a personal win.

72nd National Film Awards: Mammootty, Dhanush and Amaran win big from South The 72nd National Film Awards were announced in New Delhi on July 18. Malayalam superstar Mammootty won Best Actor, while Dhanush's Captain Miller and Sivakarthikeyan's Amaran bagged top nods for the 2024.

National Awards: Article 370 wins Best film, Rajkumar Periasamy is Best Director Article 370 won the Best Feature Film award at the 72nd National Film Awards, while Rajkumar Periasamy was honoured as Best Director for his Tamil war drama Amaran. These accolades highlight the rich storytelling and cinematic excellence celebrated across Indian cinema for the year 2024.

72nd National Awards: Mammootty, Yami Gautam, Kartik Aaryan win Best Actor honours The winners of the 72nd National Film Awards have been announced, with Yami Gautam, Mammootty and Kartik Aaryan bagging top acting honours. The prestigious awards recognised outstanding films and performances from across Indian cinema.

Batwara 1947 teaser 2 : Sunny Deol takes a stand amid partition's horror and chaos The second teaser of Batwara 1947 has been released, offering a gripping glimpse into the horrors of Partition with Sunny Deol's powerful performance and an emotional voiceover.

Actor Rajesh Sharma discharged from hospital after health scare on Fauji set Actor Rajesh Sharma has recovered after being hospitalised due to complications from an insect bite suffered while shooting for Fauji. FWICE President BN Tiwari confirmed that the actor has returned to Mumbai and is expected to resume work in the next few days.

National Film Awards 2026 winners to be announced today The Ministry of Information and Broadcasting will announce the 72nd National Film Awards in New Delhi today. The ceremony will live stream on PIB's YouTube channel.

The India Story trailer: Kajal Aggarwal, Shreyas Talpade battle pesticide crisis The trailer of The India Story: Slow Poison in Progress is out. Starring Shreyas Talpade and Kajal Aggarwal, the film follows a father and a lawyer confronting the fallout of pesticide farming.

Why Haiwaan could be Saif Ali Khan's boldest reinvention yet Haiwaan reunites actor Saif Ali Khan with director Priyadarshan as he plays a visually challenged protagonist. The role could recast the mainstream Hindi action hero through vulnerability, instinct and empathy.

Why cry? Annu Kapoor backs Satluj takedown, urges makers to seek legal route Actor Annu Kapoor has backed the government's stand on Diljit Dosanjh's Satluj and said the makers should move the Supreme Court. His remarks come as the film's removal from ZEE5 has intensified debate over censorship and creative freedom.

Varanasi: Priyanka Chopra's fierce look as Mandakini revealed on birthday SS Rajamouli unveiled Priyanka Chopra's first look as Mandakini from Varanasi on her birthday. The reveal underlines her central role in the globe-spanning epic.

Why Cillian Murphy felt relief, not regret, over missing Nolan's The Odyssey Actor Cillian Murphy said he felt 'relief' about not joining Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey. He called the absence a gift because it let him watch the film purely as an audience member.

general news.headline: Vijay govt hikes TASMAC staff salaries by 25%, first revision in 20 yearsThe Tamil Nadu government has announced a major pay hike for TASMAC employees, linking it directly to efforts to curb corruption and overcharging. But will the move end complaints from liquor buyers?

The daring story behind PM Modi's praise for Biju Patnaik in IndonesiaToday, many remember Biju Patnaik as the former Chief Minister of Odisha. But before that, Patnaik was an ace pilot who played crucial roles during the Indian and Indonesian freedom struggles. Read on to find out why PM Modi praised Patnaik in Indonesia.

Video: Miraculous escape for bystanders as landslide sweeps fuel tanker in WayanadWayanad: The landslide, which struck near Meenakshi Bridge following incessant rainfall, has claimed at least three lives, injured eight people and left seven workers missing, officials said.

E20 can damage rubber parts in older cars, says ARAI report that wasn't made publicThe use of E20 petrol in E10-compatible vehicles can damage fuel-system rubber parts, found a study by the Automotive Research Association of India (ARAI). The report, which was not made public, however, backed the use of E20 fuel. The findings align with the concerns expressed by owners of pre-2023 vehicles.

Khamenei funeral: Iran thanks India, says US threats made 13 countries stay awayIran thanked India for attending Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's state funeral in Tehran. The message came even as Iran's semi-official Tasnim News Agency said that US pressure led at least 13 countries, including two Gulf nations, to scale back or cancel participation.

Beyond war and mourning Khamenei, Iranians begin finding their new normalTehran is returning to daily life days after Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's funeral drew millions. The calmer streets mask heightened security and persistent fears of a wider regional escalation.

How Bangladesh, China are putting India to a big Teesta testDuring Bangladesh PM Tarique Rahman's China visit, Beijing agreed to accelerate a feasibility study for the Teesta river project near India's Siliguri Corridor. China had been angling for it for years but was held back by the Sheikh Hasina government's caution. That's no longer the case. This is why India will be looking closely at this development.

Exclusive: Taliban jabs Trump, says US Prez will get Bagram base 'in his dreams'Speaking to India Today, Muhajir Farahi, the Taliban's Minister for Information and Culture, also issued a stern warning to Pakistan, saying Afghanistan would defend its sovereignty against any external aggression.
Video: Delhi family attacked with sticks in road rage, cops film incidentA father and son allegedly driving on the wrong side attacked another car's occupants in East Delhi's Preet Vihar.

Quarterfinals live score: Sinner vs Struff before Djokovic takes centre stageWimbledon 2026: Defending champion Jannik Sinner will be in action on Court 1, taking on Jan Lennard Struff of Germany in the first men's singles quarterfinals. Later, Novak Djokovic will be in action on Centre Court against Felix Auger-Aliassime.

3 dead after landslide hits construction site in Wayanad, several feared trappedA major landslide struck near a tunnel project site in Kerala's Wayanad, with around seven people believed to be trapped. The incident, which occurred near Mundakkai, has revived memories of the 2024 disaster.

Caught on dashcam: Men swarm car, attack family on expressway; 3 arrestedA family was attacked after a group of men intercepted their car on the Bengaluru–Mysuru Expressway. A dashcam video of the assault has gone viral as police are yet to announce any action.

Is 'wildflowering' the right way to date?It's got nothing to do with gardening, picking flowers together or exploring the wild. Hint: It's a low-key, refreshing term in the otherwise "new toxic dating trend" buzz. Read on to find out the good, and the less good part about it.

Red alert in Delhi as rain batters capital, tree crushes vehiclesIn Delhi's East of Kailash, a large tree crashed onto parked vehicles as the national capital was lashed by rain and gusty winds on Tuesday. The IMD also issued a red alert for heavy rainfall in Delhi.

Rape-murder, lynching, Mamata 'confined': Why Baruipur case is BJP's 1st testThe rape and murder of a 12-year-old in Baruipur has posed the first major challenge for the Suvendu Adhikari government. Beyond ensuring a swift investigation, the BJP must also counter 'street fighter' Mamata Banerjee, who has seized on the incident to reclaim political ground lost.

Have ethanol protests pushed Centre's E25 plans further into future?After protests online and offline, the government seems to be going slow on its plans of higher blends of ethanol in petrol. However, for protesters like Tehseen Poonawalla, the reported rethink on E25 is just the "first victory". The government has to make suitable fuel available for all vehicles, they say.

Watch: Twin tornadoes dance over central China, kill at least 11Two tornadoes ripped through China's Hubei province, killing 11 people and injuring over 330. Here's what caused the rare and destructive weather event.

Did the Sutlej once feed the lost Saraswati? What scientists discovered over timeDid the Sutlej once flow into the legendary Saraswati River? Scientists have spent decades studying sediments, satellite imagery and ancient river channels to answer that question. Here's what geological research suggests about the Sutlej's ancient path and why it remains central to one of India's biggest historical mysteries.

China may restrict India, other countries from using its top AI modelsChina is considering restricting overseas access to its most advanced AI models, a move that could impact developers and businesses in India and other countries. If implemented, the policy could reshape the global AI race by limiting access to some of China's fastest-growing and most affordable AI systems.

Live: PM Modi to address Indian community in Jakarta shortlyPM Modi Indonesia visit Live Updates: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has begun a three-nation tour of Indonesia, Australia and New Zealand to strengthen cooperation in security, trade, technology and the Indo-Pacific. He arrived in Indonesia on the first leg of the visit. In a special gesture, President Subianto personally received Modi at the airport along with four Indonesian ministers. The Prime Minister was accorded a traditional ceremonial welcome, a Guard of Honour, and escorted by Indonesian Air Force fighter jets after entering the country's airspace. This is Modi's first bilateral visit to Indonesia since ties were elevated to a Comprehensive Strategic Partnership in 2018. PM Modi held talks with Indonesian President Prabowo Subianto and also addressed the Indonesian Parliament. The visit aims to reinforce India's Act East Policy, MAHASAGAR Vision and commitment to a free, open and inclusive Indo-Pacific. From Indonesia, Modi will travel to Melbourne at the invitation of Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese. The final leg of the tour will take him to Auckland at the invitation of New Zealand Prime Minister Christopher Luxon.Catch all the live updates at Indiatoday.in.

Ahmed Khan: Social media can make or break a film before it's even overWelcome to the Jungle director Ahmed Khan said instant social media reactions can shape a film's fate before it ends. He said word of mouth still matters most, citing the Akshay Kumar starrer's rising response.

Teen stabbed to death in Bengal's Baruipur days after minor's rape-murderAccording to local sources, the boy was murdered following tensions that had been brewing since a football match played around three days ago, in which his team had emerged victorious.

Rahul Mishra's Devi had Isha Ambani, Cardi B looking like living sculpturesCardi B and Isha Ambani attended Rahul Mishra's Paris Haute Couture Week showcase, both dressed in striking looks from the designer's latest collection.

IIIT Bengaluru student bags Rs 1.5 crore package, beats many IIT offersIIIT Bengaluru announced its highest-ever placement offer of Rs 1.5 crore at its 26th convocation. The ceremony also recognised academic excellence and other achievements, with twin brothers receiving their MTech degrees and emerging as one of the highlights of the event.

sports.headline: WWE debut, NBA audition. Novak Djokovic leaves fans speechless in New York Novak Djokovic turned Fanatics Fest in New York into his own crossover stage with a WWE-style entrance, clean three-pointers and a UFC power test. Djokovic interviewed Lionel Messi on Day one of the event.

Japan Open final live: PV Sindhu aim to end 2-year title wait PV Sindhu vs Akane Yamaguchi live, Japan Open 2026 final: PV Sindhu will be looking to end a two-year wait for a title as she faces Akane Yamaguchi in the final of the Japan Open 2026. Sindhu has been in fine form during the tournament and will be looking to get one over her familiar foe in the form of the third seed Yamaguchi.

Argentina vs Spain: Will storms or wildfire smoke affect FIFA World Cup final? Canadian wildfire smoke and severe thunderstorms raised concerns ahead of the FIFA World Cup final between Argentina and Spain, but meteorologists believe the same storms disrupting preparations will clear the air, paving the way for favourable conditions at kick-off.

One match won't tarnish Didier Deschamps' legacy: Mbappe as France finish fourth Kylian Mbappe admitted France wanted to give Didier Deschamps a winning farewell but said a disastrous first half against England left Les Bleus chasing the game. The captain insisted the defeat would not diminish Deschamps' remarkable legacy.

England's win over France is first step towards football's elite: Thomas Tuchel Thomas Tuchel believes England's dramatic 6-4 win over France is the first major step towards closing the gap on international football's elite. The England boss praised his players' mentality after securing the World Cup bronze medal.

Too little, too late? England and France save their best for 3rd place thriller FIFA World Cup 2026: Nobody wanted to play for third place, yet England and France produced the World Cup's most entertaining match. Bukayo Saka's hat-trick sealed a breathless 6-4 win, while Kylian Mbappe rewrote the record books despite finishing on the losing side.

FRA 4-6 ENG Highlights: Bellingham ends goal-fest as England win bronze France vs England Highlights, FIFA World Cup 3rd place play-off: (FRA 4-5 ENG) England defeat France 6-4 in a breathtaking third-place playoff as Bukayo Saka scores a hat-trick and Jude Bellingham seals victory in stoppage time.

Should've offered you a better ending but we failed: Mbappe's tribute to Deschamps Hours before France's World Cup third-place playoff against England, Kylian Mbappe paid an emotional tribute to outgoing coach Didier Deschamps, saying Les Bleus had "failed" to give him the farewell he deserved after 14 years at the helm.

World Cup: Rooney keeps Haaland's promise, rows across Hudson like a Viking Wayne Rooney has finally paid up after losing a playful FIFA World Cup bet to Erling Haaland. The former England captain swapped the BBC studio for a rowing boat on New York's Hudson River, honouring a promise he made after doubting Norway's chances of reaching the quarter-finals.

PV Sindhu in first final in 2 years: When and where to watch Japan Open PV Sindhu has entered the final of Japan Open. This is Sindhu's first final appearance in two years. Want to know when and where to watch her match against Akane Yamaguchi? Check out all the details here.

Rafael Nadal hopes Spain can relive 2010 magic in World Cup final vs Argentina FIFA World Cup 2026: Rafael Nadal has sent Spain an emotional message before the World Cup final against Argentina. His recall of the 2010 triumph has added to the support gathering around Luis de la Fuente's side.

Indian football is in free-fall. How to save it?Former India coach Stephen Constantine has warned that Indian football is in "free-fall", calling for structural reforms and questioning the AIFF's long-term vision after being overlooked for a third stint with the national team.

Retirement drama gone, Rohit and Gambhir share a laugh in Lord's balconyRohit Sharma and Gautam Gambhir were seen sharing a good laugh on the Lord's balcony. Rumours were rife ahead of the 3rd ODI that the Indian team management wanted to move on from Rohit.

India hit by fresh injury setback as Washington Sundar ruled out of Lord's ODIENG vs IND, 3rd ODI: India suffered another injury blow ahead of the series-deciding Lord's ODI after Washington Sundar was ruled out with a hamstring injury sustained in Cardiff.

India's Minerva Academy defend World Youth Cup, complete European doubleDays after conquering the Helsinki Cup, Minerva Academy retained the Gothia Cup by edging Brazilian side RS Sports Yellow 2-1 in another historic triumph.

Mirabai Chanu, Lovlina Borgohain to lead India at CWG 2026 opening ceremonyOlympic medallists Mirabai Chanu and Lovlina Borgohain will lead India's 126-member contingent at the opening ceremony of the 2026 Commonwealth Games in Glasgow on July 23. The honour adds another milestone to the decorated careers of two of India's biggest sporting stars.

You use Donald Trump as a witness? Thomas Tuchel hits back at England tactics digThomas Tuchel dismissed Donald Trump's criticism of his use of Harry Kane before England's third-place play-off. Trump trolled Tuchel for his defensive tactics during the semi-final loss to Argentina.

How a sex party raid nearly ended FIFA World Cup final referee's careerSlavko Vincic is all set for the biggest moment of his refereeing career as he will be the main man behind the whistle for the World Cup 2026 final between Argentina and Spain on Sunday, July 19. However, six years ago, Vincic almost had his career snatched away from him after being arrested during a sex party raid.

Djokovic asks Messi about handling pressure. Argentina star's response is goldAt Fanatics Fest in Manhattan, Novak Djokovic asked Lionel Messi how he handles pressure. The answer from the Argentina superstar won hearts and showed how Messi remained true to himself throughout his footballing journey.

Not Jannik Sinner, Carlos Alcaraz: Novak Djokovic names his biggest rival on courtNovak Djokovic said on CBS Mornings that his biggest rival has always been himself. His remarks also highlighted how ageing, recovery and crowd support are shaping this stage of his career.

Look at Messi: Ex-cricketer explains why India need Ro-Ko at World Cup 2027Mohammed Kaif said India should take Virat Kohli and Rohit Sharma to the 2027 ODI World Cup, using Lionel Messi as his example. He argued that experience will matter in South African conditions even as questions persist over Rohit's form and the pair's long-term place in the squad.

Smriti Mandhana turns 30, Jemimah celebrates their bond in emotional birthday noteSmriti Mandhana celebrated her 30th birthday with an emotional message from Jemimah Rodrigues, who reflected on the deep bond they have built over the years.

ODI World Cup: Associate captains fear ICC is closing doors on emerging teamsThe ICC's revamped format for the 2027 ODI World Cup has drawn sharp criticism from Associate captains Scott Edwards, Gerhard Erasmus, and Richie Berrington, who believe it makes qualification even tougher for emerging nations.

France vs England, 3rd place preview: The heavyweight clash no one wantedA clash worthy of a World Cup final, but with only bronze on the line. France and England meet in Miami as Didier Deschamps says goodbye, Thomas Tuchel looks for answers and Kylian Mbappe's availability remains in the spotlight.

Kohli leads with touching tribute as cricketing world mourns Sir Garfield SobersVirat Kohli paid an emotional tribute to Sir Garfield Sobers after the West Indies legend passed away at the age of 89 on Thursday, July 17.

Messi reacts to viral bathtub pic with baby Lamine Yamal. Here's what he saidLionel Messi told Tom Brady the story of his iconic bathtub photoshoot with Lamine Yamal ahead of facing the Spanish prodigy in the World Cup 2026 final. Messi met baby Yamal almost 20 years ago during a photoshoot for UNICEF.

Chocolate medal: France, England have no interest in playing 3rd place tieFrance and England go into the third-place play-off admitting neither side wants it. Didier Deschamps and Thomas Tuchel made their feelings clear that it is just business for them at the moment, and they would rather play the final.

Japan Open 2026: Fiery PV Sindhu storms into first final in 2 yearsA fiery PV Sindhu produced one of her best performances in recent times as she beat arch-nemesis Chen Yufei to storm into the final of the Japan Open 2026. This ended a two-year wait for Sindhu, who last played a final in 2024.

Donald Trump trolls Tuchel over Harry Kane call in England's semi-final lossDonald Trump mocked Thomas Tuchel's decision to drop Harry Kane into a defensive role during England's semi-final defeat to Argentina. Tuchel came under heavy criticism for his tactics during England's loss in the semi-final.

Spain's Aymeric Laporte feels referees should stop Argentina's physical approachSpain defender Aymeric Laporte has urged referees to keep Argentina's physical approach under control in the FIFA World Cup final. Speaking to Marca, Laporte said officials must ensure the match remains about football and not reckless challenges.

The greatest chapter of my career: Deschamps reflects before France farewellDidier Deschamps called the French national team the "greatest thing" of his professional career as he reflected on his 25-year journey with Les Bleus ahead of his final match in charge against England.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
